message:
fix(actrc): prevent UID mismatch when act writes files

The catthehacker/ubuntu runner images run as UID 1001 (runner).
When act uses --bind to mount the repo, any files written by the
container are created with UID 1001 instead of the host user.

This caused .beads/ directories and other files to become inaccessible
to other processes running as the host user (ubuntu, UID 1000).

Fix: Add --container-options --user=1000:1000 to run containers as
the host user, ensuring all files are created with correct ownership.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -16,6 +16,12 @@
 # Bind host Docker socket for Docker-in-Docker workflows
 --bind
 
+# CRITICAL: Run container as host user to prevent UID mismatch!
+# catthehacker images run as UID 1001 (runner). Without this line,
+# any files written by act will be owned by UID 1001, not your user.
+# Replace 1000:1000 with your actual UID:GID (run `id` to check)
+--container-options --user=1000:1000
+
 # Don't auto-remove containers (helps debugging)
 # --rm=false
